sections.paragraph:
ταλαίπωρος, ον (s. prec. entry; τ. approaches Homeric δειλό; Pind. et al.; UPZ 110, 132 [164 b.c.]; APF 5, 1913, 381 no. 56, 9 [I a.d.]; Sb 643, 8 [ostracon, Christian]; LXX; TestAbr B 10 p. 115, 4 [Stone p. 78]; JosAs 6:5, 7; ApcSed 11:15 p. 134, 34 and 36 Ja.; EpArist 130; Philo; Jos., Ant. 11, 1; Ar. 12, 8 prayers for vengeance fr. Rheneia [Dssm., LO 352; 354; 356, LAE 413ff; SIG 1181, 4f]; antonym of μακάριο) miserable, wretched, distressed w. ἐλεεινός and other adjs. Rv 3:17. ταλαίπωρος ἐγὼ ἄνθρωπος wretched creature that I am Ro 7:24 (Epict. 1, 3, 5 τί γὰρ εἰμί; ταλαίπωρον ἀνθρωπάριον ‘What am I? Just a miserable particle of humanity’; ZPE 4, ’69, 206: Fgm. B 1, 5 of a romance ὦ ταλαίπωρε ἄνθρωπε [restored]; ibid. line 9 ὁ μισητὸς ἔφη ἐγώ); cp. Hs 1:3. In the latter pass. w. δίψυχος, and also of a doubter in the scripture quot. of uncertain origin 1 Cl 23:3=2 Cl 11:2; cp. 2 Cl 11:1. Subst. (Demosth. 18, 121 ὦ ταλαίπωρε; likewise Diogenes the Cynic in Diog. L. 6, 66; Iren. 1, 13, 2 [Harv. I 117, 10]) οἱ ταλαίπωροι wretched people (Epict. 3, 22, 44) B 16:1.—S. Eranos 29, ’32, 3; 4.—Schmidt, Syn. IV 443–50. DELG. M-M. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

ZPE ZPE = Zeitschrift für Papyrologie und Epigraphik—Lists 3, 4, 6

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hs Hs = Similitudes

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

quot. quot. = quotation

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Diogenes Diogenes = letters attributed to the philosopher D. of Sinope; date uncertain—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

Eranos Eranos , Acta Philologica Suecana—List 6

Schmidt, Syn. Schmidt, Syn. = JS., Synonymik—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 988
